$meta.step: Step 2 — Design System and UX Foundation
$meta.description: Semantic breakpoints and the two hard width guarantees.

breakpoint.compact.min.description: Lower bound of the compact breakpoint. Handsets. Single column. Bottom navigation on Android. The Public Tracking Portal must remain fully usable at 320px within this range.

breakpoint.compact.max.description: Upper bound of the compact breakpoint.

breakpoint.medium.min.description: Lower bound of the medium breakpoint. Large handsets in landscape and small tablets. Navigation rail replaces bottom navigation where appropriate.

breakpoint.medium.max.description: Upper bound of the medium breakpoint.

breakpoint.expanded.min.description: Lower bound of the expanded breakpoint. Tablets and small laptops. Console Web must be fully usable at 1366x768 with no horizontal scrolling on primary workflows.

breakpoint.expanded.max.description: Upper bound of the expanded breakpoint.

breakpoint.wide.min.description: Lower bound of the wide breakpoint. Desktop. Content is capped at size.maxWidth.content and centred.

breakpoint.minSupportedWidth.description: Narrowest viewport the product supports. The Public Tracking Portal must be fully usable here, because it is the surface most often opened on an old handset from a WhatsApp message.

breakpoint.consoleReferenceWidth.description: Reference laptop width for Console Web. Primary workflows must not require horizontal scrolling at this width; large data tables use the documented responsive table pattern instead.
